title: Apple launches its lowest priced MacBook. Why it’s such a big deal.
snippet: The MacBook Neo is less expensive than the iPhone 17.

title: Apple unveils MacBook Neo, brings premium features to a lower price segment: Check India price, launch details
snippet: Apple introduced the MacBook Neo on 4 March, priced from $599, targeting budget-conscious consumers. Powered by the A18 Pro chip, it features a 13-inch Liquid Retina display and offers up to 16 hours of battery life.&nbsp;

title: Apple unveils $599 MacBook Neo with iPhone A18 Pro chip in budget laptop push
snippet: The MacBook has a 13.0-inch screen, making it one of the company’s smallest laptops to date. The MacBook Air, in contrast, offers a 13.6-inch display. Another twist: The Neo runs on an iPhone A18 Pro chip, marking the first time Apple is using a smartphone processor in a Mac.

title: Oppo confirms global rollout for Find X9 Ultra, teases advanced Hasselblad camera system: here's what to expect

title: Mass exodus looms for OpenAI as 2.5 million users vow to boycott ChatGPT after DoD deal: Report
snippet: OpenAI faces mass subscription cancellations after signing a deal with the US government to use its AI for Pentagon projects. A boycott website claims 2.5 million users will quit ChatGPT while criticizing the company’s partnership with the Defense Department.

title: Satya Nadella reveals Microsoft Build 2026 dates, flagship developer event shifts to San Francisco
snippet: The annual Microsoft Build conference will take place on June 2-3 in San Francisco. The conference will see a keynote by the company's CEO Satya Nadella while a list of other eminent speakers are also confirmed.

title: From iPhone 17e to  MacBook Air M5: Full list of Apple products launched this week
snippet: Apple launched six new products this week including the iPhone 17e with A19 processor and the MacBook Pro and Air lineup. The company is expected to showcase these products for the first time to the press at different stores throughout the world.

title: Elon Musk's X cracks down on AI deepfakes during Israel-Iran war with new revenue sharing rules
snippet: Elon Musk's platform X is revising its revenue-sharing programme and has warned creators about AI-generated content. The company says users posting AI videos of armed conflicts without disclosure will face 90-day suspensions from the programme, and subsequent violations will lead to permanent bans.

title: Vatican warns AI could lead to 'social control'
snippet: The Vatican on Wednesday warned artificial intelligence could lead to "social control" and "manipulation" and called for more focus on human relationships to counter the dehumanising effects of digital technology. Social control increases, as does the risk of manipulation," it said.
